metadata.title: Growth Rate Is All That Survives
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = growth shape / O(n) / Big-Theta / permanent verdict; CRIMSON = constants / lower-order terms / what gets absorbed
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no formal proof of dominance, no little-o/little-omega, no historical Big-O origin. Wonder register.

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: Two programs solve the same problem. On small inputs, one wins. On large inputs, the other always wins — and buying a faster computer never reverses that outcome. The gap is the shape of their growth, not their constants.

card.copy: Growth Rate Is All That Survives

beats.narration_text: Big-O notation: T(n) is O(f(n)) means there exist constants C and n₀ such that T(n) is at most C times f(n) for all n greater than n₀. Two functions with the same growth shape differ only by constants — and constants eventually lose.

production_viz.label: Big-O definition with C and n0 labeled; T(n) curve bounded above by C·f(n) in TEAL; crossover point n0 marked

beats.narration_text: If you speed up a computer running an O(n²) algorithm by a factor of four, why can't it beat an O(n) algorithm on large inputs? What does growth shape actually mean?

card.copy: O(n) vs O(n²). 4× faster CPU running O(n²) still loses. Why? What exactly is captured by the growth shape?

beats.narration_text: T₁(n) equals n and T₂(n) equals 4n squared plus 3n. At n equals 1000: T₁ equals 1000, T₂ equals about 4 million. A four-times faster CPU running T₂ gives 1 million — still 1000 times slower than T₁.

production_viz.label: T1=n (TEAL) vs T2=4n²+3n (CRIMSON) plotted; at n=1000: T1=1000, T2=4M labeled; 4× CPU reduction still leaves T2 at 1M

beats.narration_text: Divide T₂(n) by n squared as n grows: 4n squared plus 3n over n squared equals 4 plus 3 over n. As n grows large, 3 over n vanishes. The function's shape is determined entirely by the highest-order term.

production_viz.label: T2/n² = 4 + 3/n converging to constant 4 as n grows; 3/n vanishing labeled; TEAL for surviving constant term, CRIMSON for vanishing lower-order

beats.narration_text: Big-O is an upper bound on shape. Big-Omega is a lower bound. Big-Theta means both: T is sandwiched between c₁ times f(n) and c₂ times f(n) for large n. The growth shape is exactly this growth rate.

production_viz.label: Sandwich diagram: T(n) squeezed between c1·f(n) and c2·f(n); Big-Theta band in TEAL; O upper bound and Omega lower bound labeled

beats.narration_text: Lower-order terms and constants are absorbed. Whether T(n) equals 3n squared or 100n squared or n squared plus 10n plus 500, all are Theta(n²). Constants matter in practice — but for scalability, the shape is the verdict.

production_viz.label: Three functions: 3n², 100n², n²+10n+500 — all labeled Θ(n²) with TEAL bracket; constants absorbed into CRIMSON labels that get crossed out

beats.narration_text: At n equals 10 to the 6: T₁ equals 10 to the 6. T₂ equals about 4 times 10 to the 12. A 1000-times faster CPU running T₂ gives 4 times 10 to the 9 — still 4000 times slower. The gap widens with n.

production_viz.label: At n=10^6: T1=10^6 (TEAL), T2=4×10^12 (CRIMSON); 1000× CPU reduces T2 to 4×10^9, still 4000× worse than T1

beats.narration_text: This is why algorithm choice matters more than hardware at scale. Replacing an O(n²) sort with an O(n log n) sort on a billion items provides a speedup no hardware can match. The growth shape is the permanent verdict.

production_viz.label: O(n²) vs O(n log n) on billion items: n log n wins massively; TEAL for O(n log n), CRIMSON for O(n²); permanent verdict label

beats.narration_text: O(n) versus O(n squared): buying a faster computer shrinks the constant, never the shape. Divide by the highest-order term: only the growth shape survives. Big-O bounds above, Big-Omega bounds below, Big-Theta nails both. Growth shape is the final word.

card.copy: T₂ = 4n²+3n. 4× faster CPU: still 1000× slower than T₁=n at n=1000. Divide by n²: constants vanish. Growth shape is the permanent verdict — hardware can't fix it.
card.sub: Growth shape is the final word

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
